title: eneodon urticifolia
intro: Eneodon urticifolia, also known as the "stinging nettle tree," is a plant species in the family of Rubiaceae, native to South America, particularly in the tropical regions of Colombia, Peru, and Ecuador. The plant's leaves and stems contain medicinal properties, including alkaloids and iridoid glycosides, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Eneodon urticifolia include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
